# Likelihood

Likelihood connects observed data to the parameters of a statistical model. It sits
behind parameter estimation, profile constructions, and many common test statistics.

At the scaffold level it is enough to keep three points in view:

- likelihood is treated as a function of parameters for fixed data;
- the log-likelihood is usually the more practical object;
- the local curvature near the maximum is tied to the precision of the estimate.

::: {.notebook-card}
### Companion notebook

The chapter notebook builds a simple binomial log-likelihood and shows what its
one-parameter profile looks like.

<div class="notebook-actions">
  <a class="notebook-link notebook-link-primary" href="../notebooks/likelihood_demo.qmd">Open notebook</a>
</div>
:::
